failure_story: Oil and gas activity slows while produced-water growth normalizes; the market multiple compresses, service growth absorbs more capital than expected, and uncontracted infrastructure options fail to produce customer cash.
earliest_warning_signals: Royalty revenue and well inventory fall below the low-case path for two quarters.
Operated-water capital grows faster than after-tax owner cash.
No disclosed anchor customer or Phase 2B commercial validation by the next evidence refresh.
forensic_checks: Reconcile total NRA to producing and undeveloped inventory.
Audit water revenue, volumes, margins, capex, and intercompany classifications.
Trace every option to executed rights, beneficiary, remaining capital, and first falsifier.
unresolved_items: No tract/county inventory reconciles total NRA to producing, visible undeveloped, and dormant buckets.
No complete public royalty-versus-operated-water volume, margin, and capital bridge exists.
Bolt and desalination lack the complete enforceable milestone, ownership, and remaining-capital record needed for decision-grade option values.
